'''Peter Cusack''' (born 27 January 1977) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country New South Wales]] representative {{rlp|pr}}, Cusack played his club football in Australasia's [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], and in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull FC]].

==Early life==
Born in [[Young, New South Wales]], Cusack was educated at St. Gregory's College in [[Campbelltown, New South Wales]]. He was discovered by [[Arthur Beetson]] during a game for their 1st team and trialed with the [[Sydney Roosters]].

==Playing career==
Cusack made his [[National Rugby League]] premiership début with the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[1998 NRL season]]. A [[Rugby league positions|front-rower]], Cusack was one of the last remaining top-level league players to hold a job outside football, working part-time as a [[plumber]].{{citation needed|date=June 2007}} He played in the [[2000 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]]. Cusack was awarded the 2002 [[Sydney Roosters]] season's ''Clubman of the Year'' and played in their [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. He was also a member of the Roosters side that won the [[2003 World Club Challenge]], and appeared in the [[2003 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Penrith Panthers]]. Cusack played for the Roosters from the interchange bench in their [[2004 NRL grand final]] loss to cross-Sydney rivals, [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|title=Men's Honour Roll|website=Sydney Roosters|access-date=23 January 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090103/https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2005, Cusack joined the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], that year winning the [[George Piggins Medal]] as the club's best and fairest player for the season.<ref>{{cite web| title =Club records| work =rabbitohs.com.au| publisher =[[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]| year =2007| url =http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/The-Club/Tradition/Club-Records.html| accessdate =2009-11-30| url-status =dead| archiveurl =https://web.archive.org/web/20120319204248/http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/The-Club/Tradition/Club-Records.html| archivedate =19 March 2012}}</ref> He served as a co-captain at the club, along with [[Roy Asotasi]] and [[David Kidwell]].

In the [[2006 NRL season]], Cusack played 24 games as South Sydney finished last on the table.  The season also saw Souths lose 66–0 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] which is the club's worst ever defeat since entering the competition in 1908.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|date=28 June 2016|access-date=6 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In November, 2007 Cusack signed to play in the [[Super League]] with English club [[Hull F.C.]] on a two-year contract. He retired at the end of 2010 season.<ref>{{cite news|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|title=Souths release Cusack|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,22803328-23214,00.html|accessdate=2007-11-22|date=2007-11-22}}</ref>
